inline_comments.message:
Better rename the parameter to be vnfsdkPostgres and user the simpler: "{{ .Values.vnfsdkPostgres.nameOverride }}
This is the convention used everywhere else...

This POSTGRES_SERVICE_HOST, along with following environment variables are accessible by the pod, automatically : 
POSTGRES_IP=vnfsdk-postgres.onap
POSTGRES_SERVICE_PORT_HTTP=5432
POSTGRES_PORT_5432_TCP_ADDR=10.43.151.226
POSTGRES_SERVICE_HOST=10.43.151.226
POSTGRES_PORT_5432_TCP_PORT=5432
POSTGRES_PORT_5432_TCP_PROTO=tcp
POSTGRES_SERVICE_PORT=5432
POSTGRES_PORT=tcp://10.43.151.226:5432
POSTGRES_PORT_5432_TCP=tcp://10.43.151.226:5432

We don't need to define it here. 
All we need to do is to use POSTGRES_SERVICE_HOST to communicate with DB, see if it works.
